texto: About a year ago, an entire suburb in this man’s jurisdiction turned from the police and erected a substitute agency. To all intents and purposes, several thousand people here have severed their relationship with the South African Police Service. He appears not to have noticed.
explicacao: Citação de Jonny Steinberg sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “About a year ago, an entire suburb in this man’s jurisdiction turned from the police and erected …” articula reflexao, crime, police em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como police e south-africa.

texto: Difficult times do not produce gentility, as if there is an angle hovering over the world of the oppressed.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jonny Steinberg: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Difficult times do not produce gentility, as if there is an angle hovering over the world of the …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, apartheid, oppression pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de oppression e violence.

texto: There are not so many murders in this township, I think to myself, and not so few policemen, that a killing should be treated like an old woman who has lost her cat.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jonny Steinberg: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “There are not so many murders in this township, I think to myself, and not so few policemen, that…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, crime, murder pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de murder e police.

frase_original: There are not so many murders in this township, I think to myself, and not so few policemen, that a killing should be treated like an old woman who has lost her cat.

texto: Constable N stepped away from the car, into the darkness where Darren could not see where his gun was pointing, and fired two rounds into the air. The gunshots cracked the roof of the night sky and echoed back at us. My first thought was that they could be heard all over Toekomsrus; I wondered how many imaginations had in that instant conjured a different story to explain the gunshots; a record of all those stories, I found myself thinking, would probably document every fear this place has of itself and its young men.
